perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information does not include information, technical data or know -how which: (i) is in the possessi on of the receiving party at the time of disclosure as shown by the receiving party's files and records immediatel y prior to the time of disclosure; (ii) prior or after the time of disclosu re becomes part of the public knowledge or literature, not as a result of any inaction or action of the receivi ng party; or (iii) is ap proved for release by the Discloser in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information does not include information, technical data or know -how which: (i) is in the possessi on of the receiving party at the time of disclosure as shown by the receiving party's files and records immediatel y prior to the time of disclosure; (ii) prior or after the time of disclosu re becomes part of the public knowledge or literature, not as a result of any inaction or action of the receivi ng party; (iii) is ap proved for release by the Discloser in writing; or (iv) is discovered by the Recipient through independent research or analysis, even if it relates to the Discloser's business.
perturbation.explanation: Adding clause (iv) to Section 2 broadens the definition of what is *not* considered Confidential Information. However, Section 3 states that the Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that set forth in Section 1. Now, with the additional clause, the Recipient can argue that information independently discovered, even if related, is not Confidential Information and can be used for purposes outside the agreement, contradicting the non-use clause in Section 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient agr ees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Informati on to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to carry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. Recipient has had or will have directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient to whom Confidential Information is disclosed or who have access to Confidential Information sign a Non-Di sclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement and will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the names of each such person who has signed such agreements after such agreements are signed. Recipient agrees that it will take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have any such information, which measures shall include the highest degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information which may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds clause (iv), which excludes information discovered through independent research from being considered Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the original text of Section 3, which states that the Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that set forth in Section 1. Because information independently discovered is now defined as not Confidential Information, the recipient can use it for any purpose, creating a clear contradiction. This is a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction as a clause is added which now makes two locations contradict each other.

perturbation.original_text: The foregoing commitments shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties, and shall continue for a period of three (3) years following the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The foregoing commitments shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties, and shall continue indefinitely unless otherwise determined by the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: Section 7 originally set a fixed three-year term. Now, the addition of "unless otherwise determined by the Recipient" allows the Recipient to unilaterally terminate the agreement at any time, even before the three-year period, contradicting the initial fixed term. This undermines the Discloser's expectation of a guaranteed three-year protection period and thus the duration term commitment for both parties has been changed to the Recipient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The foregoing commitments shall survive any termination of discussions between the parties, and shall continue indefinitely unless otherwise determined by the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a fixed term of three years. The changed text modifies this to an indefinite term that can be terminated at any time by the Recipient. This directly contradicts the initial fixed term because the Recipient now has the unilateral ability to end the agreement before the original three-year commitment, creating an in-text contradiction as the duration term commitment for both parties has been changed to the Recipient.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that its obliga tions hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, and expressly agrees that monetary damages would be inadequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. A ccordingly, Recipient agrees an d acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause irreparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, th e Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, without the necessity of proving actual damages.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees that its obliga tions hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, however monetary damages shall be considered adequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. A ccordingly, Recipient agrees an d acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause reparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, th e Discloser shall not be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, because actual damages must be proved.
perturbation.explanation: Section 10 originally stated that monetary damages were inadequate and injunctive relief was available without proving actual damages. By stating monetary damages *are* adequate and injunctive relief is *not* available, the Recipient is directly negating the Discloser's original remedy provisions. This significantly weakens the Discloser's ability to enforce the NDA because they would have to prove damages, which are very difficult to quantify in the context of an NDA violation and no injunctive relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that its obliga tions hereunder are necessary and reasonable in order to protect the Discloser and its business, however monetary damages shall be considered adequate to compensate the Discloser for any breach of any covenants and agreements set forth herein. A ccordingly, Recipient agrees an d acknowledges that any such violation or threatened violation will cause reparable injury to the Discloser and that, in addition to any other remedies that may be available, in law, in equity or otherwise, th e Discloser shall not be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against the threatened breach of this Agreement or the continuation of any such breach, because actual damages must be proved.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. It first states that monetary damages are adequate, then states irreparable injury will occur, and then states the discloser is not entitled to injunctive relief because actual damages must be proved. This contradicts the standard for irreparable harm and injunctive relief, as actual damages are required for monetary relief, not injunctive relief. Also, the changed text directly contradicts the original text, which says that monetary damages were inadequate and injunctive relief was available without proving actual damages. By stating monetary damages *are* adequate and injunctive relief is *not* available, the Recipient is directly negating the Discloser's original remedy provisions as explained.

perturbation.original_text: The federal and state courts within the State of California shall have excl usive jurisdiction to adjudicate any dispute arising out of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The federal and state courts within the State of California shall have non-excl usive jurisdiction to adjudicate any dispute arising out of this Agreement. Disputes can also be resolved through binding arbitration in a location chosen by the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: Section 9 specified exclusive jurisdiction in California courts. The new language makes jurisdiction non-exclusive and introduces binding arbitration at the Recipient's chosen location. This allows the Recipient to force arbitration in a location unfavorable to the Discloser, undermining the Discloser's original expectation of litigating disputes in California, thus is undermining the Discloser party agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the intern al laws of the State of California, and shall be binding upon the parties hereto in The United States of America and worldwide. The federal and state courts within the State of California shall have non-excl usive jurisdiction to adjudicate any dispute arising out of this Agreement. Disputes can also be resolved through binding arbitration in a location chosen by the Recipient.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified 'exclusive jurisdiction' in California courts. The changed text modifies this to 'non-exclusive jurisdiction' and introduces binding arbitration at the Recipient's chosen location. The contradicted text contains the changed text making the jurisdiction non-exclusive and introducing binding arbitration. This directly contradicts the original intent of exclusive jurisdiction in California courts as previously established and thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. This change permits dispute resolution outside of California, which undermines the Discloser's original expectation.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that it will take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have any such information, which measures shall include the highest degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees that it will take only minimal measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have any such information, and such measures need not exceed the degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature.
perturbation.explanation: Section 3 previously required "all reasonable measures" and "the highest degree of care." Now, only "minimal measures" are required, and those measures need not exceed the Recipient's own protection standards. This greatly weakens the protection afforded to the Discloser's Confidential Information, as the Recipient may have lax internal security, thus making the level of protection not at all comparable to having the highest care.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient agr ees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Informati on to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to carry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. Recipient has had or will have directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient to whom Confidential Information is disclosed or who have access to Confidential Information sign a Non-Di sclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement and will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the names of each such person who has signed such agreements after such agreements are signed. Recipient agrees that it will take only minimal measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have any such information, and such measures need not exceed the degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information which may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The original text required "all reasonable measures" and "the highest degree of care" to protect confidential information. The changed text replaces this with "only minimal measures" and states that these measures "need not exceed the degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information." The contradicted text relies on Recipient taking measures to protect confidential information. Because of the change, 'minimal measures' is a lower standard of care, creating a contradiction because the initial reasonable measure is no longer in place, weakening the previously agreed protections.
